title: Soothing Coughs in Older Adults: 10 Herbal Creams to the Rescue

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams have been traditionally used to help alleviate coughs due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The creams typically contain the active compounds alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, which work together to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat. The alkylamides help to reduce inflammation and congestion, while the glycosides have antiviral properties that may help combat underlying infections causing the cough. If you're experiencing a persistent cough, consider applying Echinacea purpurea cream topically to the affected area, ideally 2-3 times a day. Gently massage a small amount of cream into the skin around your throat, avoiding the eyes and mouth. You can also add a few drops of the cream to your child's chest if they're old enough to use it safely under adult supervision.  As the cream works to reduce inflammation and fight off infection, you may notice a decrease in cough frequency and intensity. The glycosides may also help to stimulate the immune system, promoting a faster recovery from the underlying illness. However, if your symptoms persist or worsen, consult a healthcare professional for further guidance and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for cough

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams, also known as peppermint creams, can help alleviate cough symptoms due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The creams contain menthol, a key constituent that helps to numb the throat and reduce inflammation, making it easier to swallow and breathe. They also contain eucalyptol, which has natural expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways. When applied topically to the chest and throat, these creams can provide quick relief from coughing, congestion, and soreness. To use Mentha x piperita creams for cough, apply a small amount to the affected area 2-3 times a day. You can do this by gently massaging the cream onto your chest and throat using a circular motion. Be sure to wash your hands after applying the cream to avoid transferring it to your eyes or other areas of your skin. It's also essential to consult with a healthcare professional if your cough persists or worsens over time, as this could be a sign of an underlying condition that requires medical attention. Using Mentha x piperita creams for cough can lead to several outcomes, including reduced coughing and congestion, relief from soreness and discomfort, and improved breathing and swallowing. You may also notice a decrease in the amount of mucus produced, making it easier to clear your airways. However, it's essential to note that these creams are not a substitute for medical treatment and should only be used in conjunction with other recommended therapies.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for cough

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams are a popular remedy for coughs, and for good reason. The key to their effectiveness lies in the presence of eucalyptol, a natural compound that helps to break down and clear mucus from the airways. Eucalyptol is a powerful expectorant, which means it helps to loosen and release mucus, making it easier to cough up. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe a sore throat and reduce congestion. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus creams often contain other beneficial compounds like menthol and camphor, which can help to cool and calm the airways. If you're experiencing a persistent cough, try applying Eucalyptus globulus cream to your chest or throat before bed. Gently rub a small amount into your skin, focusing on the areas where you feel congestion. You can also inhale the vapor from a warm bath or shower after applying the cream, to help the eucalyptol and other compounds work their way deeper into your airways.  When you take action, you can expect to start feeling relief from your cough within a few hours. The eucalyptol and other compounds in the cream will begin to break down and clear mucus from your airways, making it easier to breathe and cough up. Your throat may also start to feel soothed and less inflamed, and your congestion may begin to clear. However, if your cough persists or worsens, it's always a good idea to speak with your doctor to rule out any underlying conditions that may require medical attention.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for cough

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams are used to help soothe and calm a cough. The creams contain properties from the licorice root, which includes glycyrrhizin and flavonoids. Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and swallow. Flavonoids have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation.  When using Glycyrrhiza glabra creams for cough, apply a thin layer to the affected area, usually the throat or chest, 2-3 times a day. You can also mix a few drops of the cream with water to make a soothing gargle. If you're experiencing a dry, persistent cough, you may also consider taking the cream orally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.  When you start using Glycyrrhiza glabra creams, you may experience a reduction in coughing and a decrease in throat irritation. You may also notice improved breathing and swallowing. However, if you have high blood pressure, kidney disease, or are taking certain medications, consult a healthcare professional before using these creams, as they can interact with other substances and worsen certain conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for cough

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams, also known as thyme cream, are a popular natural remedy for coughs. The active ingredients in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the throat and calm the cough. When you apply thymus vulgaris creams to your throat, the thymol and carvacrol work to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause the cough. This can help to break the cough cycle and provide quick relief. If you're experiencing a cough, try applying thymus vulgaris creams to your throat several times a day. You can also mix a few drops of thyme essential oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it to your chest or throat. Be sure to follow the instructions on the label and start with a small amount to test for any skin irritation. When you use thymus vulgaris creams, you can expect to feel a reduction in coughing within a few hours. The inflammation in your throat should start to decrease, and you may notice that your cough becomes less frequent and less severe. In some cases, the cough may even disappear altogether. However, if your cough persists or worsens, you should consult with a healthcare professional to rule out any underlying conditions that may require medical attention.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for cough

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams, commonly known as ginger creams, can provide relief from cough due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The creams contain compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and swallow. These constituents also help to relax the muscles in the throat and chest, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing spasms. To use Zingiber officinale creams for cough, apply a small amount to the affected area, such as the throat or chest, and gently massage it in. You can do this 2-3 times a day, or as needed. It's essential to follow the product instructions and consult with a healthcare professional if you have any underlying medical conditions or allergies. When used correctly, you can expect the following outcomes: the frequency and severity of coughing may decrease, making it easier to breathe and swallow. The inflammation in your throat and airways may reduce, and you may experience relief from discomfort and congestion. However, it's crucial to note that Zingiber officinale creams may not completely eliminate the cough, especially if it's caused by a viral infection or underlying condition. In such cases, you should continue to seek medical attention and follow your healthcare professional's advice.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for cough

remedies.remedy_desc: Myrrhis odorata creams have been traditionally used to help relieve coughs. The creams contain compounds like ferulic acid, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Ferulic acid helps to reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and swallow. The creams also contain essential oils like eucalyptus and menthol, which have natural decongestant properties that help to thin and clear mucus from the lungs. To use Myrrhis odorata creams for a cough, apply a small amount to your chest or throat several times a day. Gently rub the cream into your skin until it's fully absorbed. You can also add a few drops of eucalyptus oil to a warm bath or inhale it through steam inhalation to help loosen mucus and ease congestion. When you take action and use Myrrhis odorata creams, you may start to feel relief from your cough within a few hours. Your throat and chest may feel less congested, and you may be able to breathe more easily. You may also notice that you're coughing less often, and that your mucus is thinner and easier to clear. However, it's essential to note that Myrrhis odorata creams may not work for everyone, and you should consult with a healthcare professional before using them, especially if you have a severe or persistent cough.
remedies.image_alt: Myrrhis odorata herbal creams for cough

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra creams are made from the elderflower, a plant that has been used for centuries to help with coughs and colds. The creams contain flavonoids, such as quercetin and isorhamnetin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the throat and ease congestion. They also contain sambunigrin, a compound that has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help to fight off the underlying infection causing the cough. To use Sambucus nigra creams, apply a small amount to the affected area, such as the throat, chest, or nose, and gently massage it in. You can also add a few drops of the cream to a warm bath or inhale it through steam inhalation to help loosen mucus and ease congestion. It's best to use the creams regularly, ideally 3-4 times a day, to get the full benefits. When you start using Sambucus nigra creams, you may notice a reduction in the severity of your cough, and it may become easier to breathe and sleep. Your congestion may also start to clear, and you may feel more energized and less tired. However, it's essential to note that Sambucus nigra creams are not a cure for coughs and colds, and you should also rest, stay hydrated, and use other treatments as recommended by your doctor to help manage your symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Sambucus nigra herbal creams for cough

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams can help with cough due to their calming and soothing properties. These creams contain compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. When applied topically to the chest and throat area, these compounds can help reduce inflammation and ease congestion, making it easier to breathe and relieving coughing fits. To use Lavandula angustifolia creams for cough, apply a thin layer to the affected area 2-3 times a day. You can also add a few drops of the cream to a warm bath or inhale the vapors to help relax your airways. It's essential to follow the instructions on the packaging and consult with a healthcare professional before using any new product, especially if you have underlying medical conditions or allergies. When you use Lavandula angustifolia creams, you may experience a reduction in coughing fits, improved breathing, and a sense of relief from congestion. You may also notice that your sleep improves as you're able to relax and breathe easier. However, if your cough persists or worsens, it's crucial to seek medical attention to rule out any underlying conditions that require medical treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for cough

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus creams are used to help relieve coughs, and they work by targeting the root cause of the problem. The creams contain a compound called mucilage, which is a thick, gooey liquid that comes from the plant's leaves. Mucilage helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, making it easier to swallow and reducing the urge to cough. Other constituents in the cream, such as flavonoids and saponins, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm the throat and reduce swelling. To use Verbascum thapsus creams for cough, apply a small amount to the affected area, usually the throat, 2-3 times a day. Gently massage the cream into the skin, making sure to cover the entire area. You can also mix the cream with a bit of water to make it easier to apply. Some people find that using the cream before bedtime helps to reduce coughing at night. When you apply Verbascum thapsus creams to your throat, you may start to feel a soothing sensation within a few minutes. The mucilage in the cream can help to calm the cough and reduce the amount of mucus produced. As the cream works, you may notice that your cough becomes less frequent and less severe. In some cases, people have reported that their cough completely disappears within a few hours of using the cream. However, the effectiveness of the cream can vary from person to person, and some people may need to use it for several days to see results.
remedies.image_alt: Verbascum thapsus herbal creams for cough

intro_desc: As people age, their bodies become more vulnerable to illness and injury. Older adults are more likely to suffer from cough, a common symptom of various health conditions such as colds, flu, and chronic respiratory diseases like bronchitis and pneumonia. Coughing can be a real nuisance, making it hard for older adults to sleep, eat, and even breathe. It can also lead to fatigue, dehydration, and further complications like pneumonia. For older adults with pre-existing health conditions, cough can be particularly concerning as it can worsen their condition. Moreover, cough can be a sign of an underlying condition that needs medical attention. Older adults may also experience a decline in their immune system, making them more susceptible to respiratory infections.  When it comes to treating cough, older adults often rely on over-the-counter medications, which can have side effects and interact with other medications they may be taking. This is where herbal creams come in. These creams are made from natural ingredients that are known to soothe and calm the throat, reducing inflammation and relieving cough symptoms. By applying a topical cream directly to the affected area, older adults can experience quick relief from cough without having to take medication orally. This can be especially beneficial for older adults who may be sensitive to certain ingredients or have difficulty swallowing pills. Herbal creams can provide a natural and gentle way to manage cough symptoms, giving older adults some much-needed relief and comfort.
